content:
# Final Review Proposal
**Producer:** agent:codex/gpt-5.4-xhigh

## Summary

Reviewed the rlm-5 implementation diff end-to-end across the daemon cancellation, lease cleanup, CLI recovery, and workflow interruption paths. I found 1 correctness gap: daemon cancellation still has no attempt-based fallback when `run.pid` is already missing or replaced, so it can release the task lease while leaving the project run stale (and in the graceful branch, without forcing backend-process cleanup).

## Amendments

1. Add an attempt-based fallback in daemon cancellation cleanup for cases where `run.pid` is already gone or no longer matches the current daemon process. `prepare_cancelled_dispatch_handoff()` explicitly preserves `expected_attempt` when the pid file is missing/mismatched (`src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs:2943-2961`), but both `finish_cancelled_dispatch()` branches only try `reconcile_cancelled_dispatch_run()` when `interrupted_marker_persisted` is false (`src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs:3124-3143`, `src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs:3156-3168`). That helper calls `mark_current_process_running_run_interrupted()` (`src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs:2877-2900`), which immediately returns `false` if `run.pid` is absent or no longer belongs to this process (`src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:857-889`). The daemon then surfaces `InvocationCancelled`, callers release the task lease, and the project can be left with a stale `run.json` still marked `Running`; on the grace-completed path it also skips `cleanup_cancelled_dispatch_backend_processes()`, so tracked backend groups can survive. Use the saved `expected_attempt` to reconcile/finalize interrupted state and run backend cleanup even when the pid file has already disappeared.
   Rationale: This is exactly the failure mode the bead is trying to close: daemon-owned cancellation can still leave stale durable run state, and sometimes orphaned backend processes, when the pid file is missing or has already changed by the time shutdown cleanup runs.
